ops.text:
## E16 bias-audit guidance (ledger 10ed7d87) — folded into the Option-D forward plan

Received AFTER this loop completed; NOT a re-run trigger (the below_mde/ceiling outcome is an instrument-POWER limit — baseline 0.946, headroom 0.054 < MDE 0.108 on the golden-heavy combined metric — not a mutation-targeting limit; better-targeted mutations still can't clear MDE on this panel).

Guidance for mutation requests in any FUTURE E19 / Option-D run:
- STRONGEST signal = the noise-vs-steering CATEGORY boundary (new_task / wrong_approach / question / operational_directive). Boundary-sharpening mutations are the priority AND double as style-robustness (style churn concentrates exactly at that boundary).
- STYLE (paraphrase-robustness): the only registered E16 flag, category-metric-only, delta CI touches zero → LOW-PRIORITY, tentative at most.
- LENGTH and POSITION robustness: do NOT spend mutations — both at/below control noise.
- `direction`: robust everywhere — no mutation needed.

Retrospective validation: the completed loop's GEPA mutations (c001→c003) already concentrated on exactly this category boundary (operational_directive-vs-direction rules, source-kind-aware carve-outs) — i.e. they were already aimed at the strongest signal. The ceiling, not the aim, is why they didn't clear MDE.

Option-D forward plan therefore = (1) mint fresh independent auditor (opus) refs for the ~109 v6-only contested rows → ~125-row clean contested panel; (2) score on a CONTESTED-STRATUM metric (not golden-diluted combined) to restore power; (3) mutation requests targeting the category boundary per the E16 guidance above. Authorization + spend (~$41 labeling) pending orchestrator.
